_why: Defines the sequential decision logic executed per turn before and after the NIM inference call. Each stage has a defined skip condition to minimise unnecessary processing on simple turns.

turn_workflow.name: rpm_guard
turn_workflow.why: First gate. Reject before any processing if RPM ceiling reached. Cheapest possible check.

turn_workflow.name: credential_scrub
turn_workflow.why: Redact secrets before any external API call. Must run before profile_selector to avoid leaking tokens into NIM.

turn_workflow.name: profile_selector
turn_workflow.why: Classify task class and inject correct temperature, max_tokens, and reasoning_budget before NIM call.

turn_workflow.name: context_budget_enforcer
turn_workflow.why: Enforce 65K token ceiling before NIM call. Truncation preserves system prompt + last 3 turns + current message.

turn_workflow.name: nim_inference
turn_workflow.why: The actual NIM inference call. Streaming enabled to reduce perceived latency on Free Tier.

turn_workflow.name: response_quality_monitor
turn_workflow.why: Outlet quality scoring. Attaches _ai_os_quality metadata for benchmark harness. Zero impact on user-visible response.

tool_batching_policy.rationale: Each NIM call consumes 1 RPM. Chaining NIM→Tool→NIM doubles RPM cost per turn. All tools must be called in parallel before the single NIM inference call.

rpm_accounting.why: Only NIM API calls count against RPM. Filters and tools do not consume NIM quota.
